summary: High‑quality soundboard recording of a tight spring‑1990 show, highlighted by scorching Scarlet > Crazy Fingers, stellar Brent keyboards and Jerry’s smoking guitar.
review: The Omni 1990 run captures the Dead at a peak of tightness and improvisational flair – an unusual Estimated → Scarlet Begonias → Crazy Fingers segue, fiery Brent on keys, and a space that many call the best of the tour.  The soundboard source is generally clear and punchy, though a few listeners note first‑set muddiness and high‑end clipping.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews consistently praise the performance (many 4–5 stars) while noting only minor sound issues; weighted average of show quality (~4.5) and recording quality (~4.0) yields ~4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced mix overall; occasional first‑set muddiness, high‑frequency clipping, and background noise reported by a few listeners but not enough to detract from the experience.

standout_songs: Goin' Down the Road Feelin' Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual second‑set opener (Estimated) leads into a seamless Scarlet > Crazy Fingers sequence, keeping the momentum high; first set rocks with Shakedown Street and Sugaree, while the encore delivers a classic Bid You Goodnight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Top form throughout – smoking guitar tone, sweet phrasing on Row Jimmy and tight lead work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Locked‑in bass, heard loud and clear in several reviews.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent shines on keyboards; his playing described as “on fire” and a key highlight of the night.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s space drumming praised as one of the best ever; overall drumming called “crazy drum circles” and solid.
